bottleneck.evidence: Final evaluate.py --repeat 3 for the PDL variant: M=16 solution 811.06 us vs baseline 820.53 us, M=32 solution 812.90 us vs baseline 831.15 us; conservative min speedup is only 0.8751 because M=32 had a 943.12 us high sample. Advisory profile M=16 reports 782.82 us and 10.9% HBM, so the tiny decode sweep is not HBM-bound.

approaches.summary: Added glm52/routed_gateup_nvfp4_decode as a separate ModelOpt-FP4 task using packed uint8 NVFP4 activations and weights, FP8 E4M3 block scales, FP32 scalar scales, and FlashInfer TRT-LLM routed MoE.
approaches.why: The task contract is correct and uses the requested SGLang/FlashInfer NVFP4 backend, but task creation alone does not provide a robust conservative speedup.

approaches.summary: Set enable_pdl=True on the FlashInfer TRT-LLM routed FP4 MoE call in solution.py.
approaches.why: PDL improved median latency slightly on both shapes, but the M=32 high sample erased the conservative gate margin.

approaches.summary: Initially kept solution.py identical to the SGLang/FlashInfer reference to validate the new task contract before attempting a custom kernel.
approaches.why: The pass-through candidate was correct but slightly slower than the live reference under repeat-3 noise.

lesson: For GLM-5.2 NVFP4 MoE decode on sm_100, enabling FlashInfer PDL may shift median latency by about 1-2%, but it is not stable enough to satisfy the conservative gate on the M=16/32 sweep. The main remaining headroom is likely reducing fixed TRT-LLM runner overhead or exposing a gate-up-only primitive instead of timing the fused MoE path.
transfers_to: GLM-5.2 modelopt_fp4 MoE decode tasks on sm_100
FlashInfer TRT-LLM routed MoE tasks using packed uint8 NVFP4 plus FP8 E4M3 scales
caveats: This no-win entry supersedes glm52--routed_gateup_nvfp4_decode--b200--20260714a for the final solution.py state.
Advisory profile numbers are not used as result speedups.
